# build an epic -> task -> subtask chain and render it
mkdir proj
cd proj
exec mtt init --template hierarchy
stdout 'initialized'

exec mtt add --type epic 'E'
stdout 'created e1'
exec mtt add --type task --parent e1 'T'
stdout 'created t1'
exec mtt add --type subtask --parent t1 'S'
stdout 'created s1'

# tree renders all three nodes (presence, not order)
exec mtt tree
stdout 'e1  epic  \[tbd\]  E'
stdout 't1  task  \[tbd\]  T'
stdout 's1  subtask  \[tbd\]  S'

# rooting at t1 shows t1 + s1 but not e1
exec mtt tree t1
stdout 't1  task  \[tbd\]  T'
stdout 's1  subtask'
! stdout 'e1  epic'

# depth 1 from the root shows only e1
exec mtt tree --depth 1
stdout 'e1  epic'
! stdout 't1  task'

# --exclude-tag (c10) + keep-ancestors: an excluded PARENT is kept as the path to a
# still-visible child (exclude does not nuke the subtree)
exec mtt tag add t1 skip
exec mtt tree --exclude-tag skip
stdout 't1  task'
stdout 's1  subtask'
# but an excluded LEAF is dropped (its ancestors have no other reason to show it)
exec mtt tag add s1 gone
exec mtt tree --exclude-tag gone
! stdout 's1  subtask'
stdout 't1  task'
# tidy up so later assertions see the original tag-free chain
exec mtt tag rm t1 skip
exec mtt tag rm s1 gone

# --type / --priority / --parent close the filter parity with list (c16);
# keep-ancestors applies: ancestors stay as the path to a matching descendant
exec mtt tree --type subtask
stdout 's1  subtask'
stdout 't1  task'
exec mtt tree --type ghost
! stdout 'e1  epic'
exec mtt edit s1 --priority high
stdout 'updated s1'
exec mtt tree --priority high
stdout 's1  subtask'
stdout 'e1  epic'
exec mtt tree --priority low
! stdout 's1  subtask'
! exec mtt tree --priority bogus
stderr 'invalid'
exec mtt tree --parent t1
stdout 's1  subtask'
stdout 't1  task'
exec mtt tree --parent s1
! stdout 'e1  epic'
# tidy up the priority so later assertions see the original chain
exec mtt edit s1 --priority ''

# a missing root errors (wraps ErrNotFound → exit 4)
! exec mtt tree nope
stderr 'task "nope": mtt: task not found'

# an invalid --kind errors
! exec mtt tree --kind bogus
stderr 'invalid --kind'

# --json emits a nested tree: e1 with a children array down to s1
exec mtt tree --json
stdout '"id": "e1"'
stdout '"children"'
stdout '"id": "t1"'
stdout '"id": "s1"'

# an empty subtree filter still yields a JSON array, never null
exec mtt tree --status ghost --json
stdout '\[\]'

# show renders the lineage breadcrumb (root-to-self path) for a nested task
exec mtt show s1
stdout 's1  subtask  \[tbd\]'
stdout 'lineage:  e1 › t1 › s1'
! stdout 'parent:'

# a mid-level task shows its lineage and its children
exec mtt show t1
stdout 'lineage:  e1 › t1'
stdout 'children: 1 \(s1\)'

# a root task has no lineage line, but does list its children
exec mtt show e1
! stdout 'lineage:'
stdout 'children: 1 \(t1\)'
